_provenance: Hand-compiled from the OpenAI Apps SDK submission docs (developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/deploy/submission, /app-submission-guidelines, /guides/optimize-metadata) and the Company Dossier setup. This is NOT the output of an official OpenAI skill/plugin — none exists. The real submission is a web form at https://platform.openai.com/apps-manage; copy these values into it.
_prerequisites: Complete identity verification (individual or business) in the OpenAI Platform Dashboard.
Ensure your org/API key has the `api.apps.write` permission.

app.name: Company Dossier
app.short_description: Sourced intelligence dossiers on any company, straight from the public record.
app.long_description: Company Dossier compiles a complete, source-attributed intelligence file on any company or domain — entirely from public data. One request returns nine sections: overview & identity, people & org chart, hiring radar, money trail, locations, tech fingerprint, news & timeline, relationship web, and risk flags. Every claim is tied to a public source and tagged High/Medium/Low confidence. Built for due diligence, vendor vetting, sales prep, and competitive research. No accounts, no private data — public sources only.

auth.action_needed: Switch the endpoint to no-auth + rate-limited before submitting; a shared bearer token is not an accepted public-listing auth model.

tools.name: build_dossier
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for research, a background check, due diligence, a profile, or a 'dossier' on a specific company or domain. Returns a nine-section, source-cited report (overview, people, hiring, funding, locations, tech stack, news, relationships, risk flags) compiled from public sources, each claim confidence-tagged. Do not use for general web search, for private individuals who are not company representatives, or for non-business entities.

target.description: Company name or domain, e.g. 'Acme Robotics' or 'acme.com'.

sections.description: Optional comma-separated subset of: overview, people, hiring, money, locations, tech, news, relationships, risk. Omit for all nine.

test_prompts.expected: Calls build_dossier with target 'stripe.com'; returns the nine sourced sections.

test_prompts.prompt: Do due-diligence research on Acme Robotics
test_prompts.expected: Calls build_dossier with target 'Acme Robotics'; returns the sourced file.

screenshots._todo: Capture in ChatGPT after connecting the server in Settings > Connectors > Developer mode, then run 'Build a dossier on stripe.com' and screenshot the result.
screenshots.dimensions: as specified by the dashboard at upload (commonly ~1280x800)

steps: Add MCP server details (URL above; OAuth creds only if using OAuth).
Complete required fields: app name, logo, descriptions, company info, privacy policy URL, MCP & tool info, screenshots, test prompts + expected responses, localization, country availability.
Check all confirmation boxes acknowledging the App Submission Guidelines.
Click 'Submit for review' — you'll receive an email with a Case ID.

_known_risks: POLICY: Apps SDK guidelines prohibit apps that scrape external websites / act as unofficial connectors. This app crawls public company sites — a genuine rejection risk for the Apps SDK directory. The GPT Store (Custom GPT using ChatGPT's own browsing) is lower-risk.
AUTH: endpoint currently bearer-gated; must be no-auth or OAuth for a public listing.
